title: BOOKS Carmine Sadamotos 3rd Book
wikitext:
[]: [BOOKS] Carmine (Sadamoto's 3rd Book)             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Mar 22, 2009 11:15 am Sadamoto is putting out a third book, called CARMINE Eva Store Amazon Japan Rapetacular price since it includes Alpha and Der Mond as well as all the new stuff from what I understand. It's actually coming out in 3 days.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Mar 22, 2009 11:25 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Rapetacular price since it includes Alpha and Der Mond as well as all the new stuff from what I understand. It's actually coming out in 3 days.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 22, 2009 1:05 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Rapetacular price since it includes Alpha and Der Mond as well as all the new stuff from what I understand. It's actually coming out in 3 days.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Mar 22, 2009 2:20 pm Actually, for only 160 pages the price is a little high. Die Sterne is over 300 pages and you can find it for $40. @Reichu: Gee, Reichu. You're losing your touch!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 22, 2009 2:33 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: Gee, Reichu. You're losing your touch!

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Mar 22, 2009 2:43 pm Reichu wrote: Unless you were joking.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Mar 23, 2009 5:20 am Reichu wrote: Sadamoto’s “Carmine” out on 3/25

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Mon Mar 23, 2009 12:05 pm Doesn't Carmine also include both Alpha and Der Mond? I've got the latter, but not the former.

[]:             Quote Post by Soth » Fri Mar 27, 2009 1:39 am After reading this thread, I decided to purchase Carmine just to have Alpha and Der Mond contents compiled in a deluxe version, but it doesn't include any pic neither from Alpha nor Der Mond . Also, except if you are a collector of limited edition stuff, the price doesn't match the contents; the overall content is equivalent to the amount appeared on Der Mond and the "extras" are not worth the extra +10,000 yen. My advice is to wait for a regular edition (is Gainax after all, we all expect a normal edition anyways).

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Fri Mar 27, 2009 11:10 am Indigo Arcangel wrote: Doesn't Carmine also include both Alpha and Der Mond? I've got the latter, but not the former.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 27, 2009 1:35 pm Carmine sounds like a ripoff. SHENANIGANS!

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Mar 27, 2009 6:50 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Carmine sounds like a ripoff. SHENANIGANS!

[]:             Quote Post by Soth » Fri Mar 27, 2009 6:53 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: Indigo Arcangel wrote: Doesn't Carmine also include both Alpha and Der Mond? I've got the latter, but not the former.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 27, 2009 8:40 pm Soth wrote: Oedipusfoot wrote: Indigo Arcangel wrote: Doesn't Carmine also include both Alpha and Der Mond? I've got the latter, but not the former.

[]:             Quote Post by Soth » Fri Mar 27, 2009 10:11 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Soth wrote: Oedipusfoot wrote: Indigo Arcangel wrote: Doesn't Carmine also include both Alpha and Der Mond? I've got the latter, but not the former.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Mar 27, 2009 10:16 pm She probably got it from the product description from one of those merchant sites.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Mar 27, 2009 10:30 pm Soth wrote: Is what I'm trying to say. Reichu's statement is not true and Alpha and Der Mond are not included at all. Carmine is a brand new book with brand new contents that doesn't compile his previously released works.

[]:             Quote Post by Soth » Fri Mar 27, 2009 10:54 pm The proof: http://4.bp.blogspot.com/_iCnzf-y60cc/Sc2b4TgPLjI/AAAAAAAAAag/HigIdYmP-V8/s1600-h/DSC00630.JPG That is why I'm warning you people: is a 13,000 yen book of 166 pages (30 of them the interviews) and a bunch of useless extras that doesn't compile his previously released works. I bought it because Reichu wrote that Alpha and Der Mond were compiled (even if in the Eva Store doesn't explicitly says so) and I'm letting you know that is not true, so don't do the same mistake as I did.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Mar 27, 2009 10:57 pm But the cover looks so cool... Anyway, thanks for the heads up. (also, that pic of Rei is from the cover of Die Sterne)

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Mar 27, 2009 10:58 pm Soth wrote: You people are being pretty stubborn, huh?

It's actually coming out in 3 days.
